readingQuestions.prompt: 本文を読んで各設問に答えてください。

payload.title: The Cities Beneath the Waves
paragraphs: From the surface, a coral reef can look like a field of colourful rock. In truth, it is alive. Each tiny coral animal builds a hard cup of limestone around itself, and over thousands of years these cups join together into vast structures that shelter about a quarter of all sea creatures.

The bright colours that draw divers come from microscopic plants living inside the coral. These plants make food from sunlight and share it with their hosts, while the coral gives them a safe place to grow. It is a partnership so close that neither side can thrive well alone.

When the sea grows too warm, however, the coral pushes out its tiny partners and turns a ghostly white. If the heat passes quickly, the plants may return; if it lingers, the reef slowly starves. Scientists now watch ocean temperatures closely, knowing that a few extra degrees can decide the fate of these underwater cities.

questions.prompt: 本文の主旨として最も適切なものはどれですか。
options: A coral reef is a living, cooperative system that is full of life but easily harmed when the sea becomes too warm.
A coral reef is simply a field of colourful rock with no living creatures inside it.
Coral reefs grow faster and stronger whenever the surrounding water becomes warmer.
The plants inside coral harm the reef and should be removed to keep it healthy.

questions.before: The plants inside the coral use sunlight to make food, which they then
questions.after: with the coral that shelters them.

questions.prompt: 海水温が高くなりすぎたときに起こることを示す語句はどれですか。

spans.text: these cups join together into vast structures
make food from sunlight and share it with their hosts
the coral pushes out its tiny partners and turns a ghostly white
Scientists now watch ocean temperatures closely

questions.sentence: In this way, the animal and the plant depend on each other for their very survival.

questions.prompt: 第3段落で述べられている内容として最も適切なものはどれですか。
options: Warm water can force coral to lose its colour, and if the heat lasts, the reef may slowly die.
Warm water makes coral grow new colours and helps the reef become much larger.
Coral never reacts in any way to changes in the temperature of the sea.
Scientists have decided that ocean temperature has no effect on coral reefs.

readingQuestions.prompt: 本文を読んで各設問に答えてください。

payload.title: From Bitter Bean to Sweet Treat
paragraphs: Long before chocolate filled shop windows, it was a bitter drink. In ancient Central America, people roasted cacao beans, ground them into a paste, and mixed them with water and spices. The result was sharp and frothy, nothing like the sweet bars we know today.

When the drink finally reached Europe, cooks began adding sugar to soften its bitter taste. For a long time it remained a luxury, sipped only by the wealthy in grand houses. Making it by hand was slow work, so very few people could afford to enjoy it often.

Everything changed when machines arrived. New factories could grind the beans, add milk and sugar, and pour the mixture into moulds quickly and cheaply. Chocolate that had once been a treat for kings was suddenly within reach of ordinary families, and it has stayed popular ever since.

questions.prompt: 本文が最も伝えたいことはどれですか。
options: Chocolate began as a bitter drink and slowly became the sweet, affordable treat that almost anyone can enjoy today.
Chocolate has always been a cheap, sweet bar that everyone could easily buy.
Chocolate was first invented in modern European factories only a few years ago.
Chocolate is still a bitter drink that only very wealthy people are able to enjoy.

questions.before: In ancient Central America, the cacao drink tasted bitter because no

questions.prompt: チョコレートが安く大量に作れるようになった理由を示す語句はどれですか。

spans.text: people roasted cacao beans, ground them into a paste
sipped only by the wealthy in grand houses
New factories could grind the beans, add milk and sugar, and pour the mixture into moulds quickly and cheaply
The result was sharp and frothy

questions.prompt: 本文全体の要点はどれですか。
options: Over time, chocolate changed from a costly bitter drink into a sweet treat that ordinary people can afford.
Chocolate has become far more expensive and harder to find than ever before.
Chocolate was always sweet and was never made or served as a drink.
Only machines in ancient times were ever able to make chocolate.

listeningQuestions.transcript: Could you let me know as soon as the package arrives at the front desk?
The museum opens at nine thirty and the last tickets are sold at half past four.
Were it not for the rain, we would have finished painting the entire house today.
She promised to call me back the moment she got out of the meeting.
The clever thief slipped through the narrow gap without making a single sound.
I can't decide whether to take the bus or simply walk to the station.
